place: Blue Point CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Blue Point’s roots stretch back to the mid‑17th century, when English settlers from Connecticut and Southold Township first claimed the land in 1655.
The Bayport‑Blue Point Library began in 1938 as a Parent‑Teacher Association project, growing from donated books into a community cultural hub.
Meadow Croft, the summer estate of John Ellis Roosevelt, sits in Blue Point and now serves as a museum listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
Historic preservation thrives in Blue Point, thanks to groups like the Bayport‑Blue Point Heritage Association founded in 1983 to protect local artifacts.
Blue Point’s early settlement was shaped by its proximity to Setauket, yet permanent European homes didn’t appear until the 18th century.
The town’s library logo—a wheelbarrow holding books—honors its origins when it first opened in a room of the Blue Point School.
